meta.tagline: You can read this. Saying it is the hard part.
meta.description: An AI voice partner who remembers what you said and shows up in the gaps of your day. No booking. No camera.
meta.homeTitle: Palkie Talkie — You can read this. Saying it is the hard part.

hero.smallprint: Free {day} min/day, {week} min/week. No credit card. Cancel anytime.

visual.label: [Hero visual placeholder]
visual.spec: Phone-shaped frame with the mic-screen of the app, persona avatar mid-turn, captions wrapping. Static PNG or 5-sec looped video. Replace with a real screenshot once the app is on TestFlight.

audioSample.label: [10-second persona audio sample placeholder]
audioSample.spec: A 10s clip of the AI partner delivering an opening turn ("Hey — you got into work yet, or still on the walk over?"). Record by running one dev session against OpenAI Realtime with the test prompt, dumping the audio stream to .wav, and trimming the first 10s in Audacity. Web encodes as .mp3 (preferred) or .aac. File at /public/audio/persona-sample.mp3.

problems.title: Functional ≠ fluent.
problems.lead: You can read English. You can get through the day. The gap is between what's in your head and what comes out of your mouth — and it doesn't close on its own.

items.title: You miss the jokes
items.body: You can hold the meeting. But the side-comment that made everyone laugh? You smiled along. The casual chat after? You sounded more formal than you wanted to be. The harder you try, the more it shows.

items.title: No time to sit down and study
items.body: Work is work. What's left is fragments — the walk from the train to the office, the bike ride home, the shower, the gym. The moments you can talk out loud without being self-conscious. Most apps want a desk and 30 minutes. You don't have either.

items.title: The other tools miss the point
items.body: Tap-and-translate drills don't build speaking. Generic voice assistants reset every session and have no personality. Live tutors need a booking and a camera. None of them feel like a real conversation with someone who knows you.

absence.title: What you don't have to do.
absence.lead: The friction is the reason you stopped. Take it out.

items.title: No booking
items.body: Open it when you have a minute. Close it when you don't.

items.title: No camera
items.body: Voice only. Walk, ride, shower, talk.

items.title: No chair, no desk
items.body: Built for the gaps, not for sitting down to study.

items.title: No smalltalk filler
items.body: Never "How can I help you today?" Your AI partner already knows you.

features.title: Three things that change everything.
features.lead: Cut the cold-start, cut the reset, cut the boring tutor. Talk to an AI partner who knows you.

items.title: It talks first
items.body: Open the app and your AI partner is already speaking — addressing you by name, picking up where you left off. Answer if you want. Just listen if you don't.

visual.label: [Feature visual placeholder]
visual.spec: Avatar mid-speech, sound waveform under the captions. Square 1:1, ~400×400. Midjourney prompt: 'illustrated character, friendly expression, mid-conversation, warm background, voice-wave element subtle, square, flat illustration style'.

items.title: It remembers you
items.body: What you said last week, the joke that landed, the deadline you mentioned, the kid's name. Next conversation, it weaves them back in like a friend would — not like a chatbot reciting a profile.

visual.label: [Feature visual placeholder]
visual.spec: Two speech bubbles connected by a soft arc — Friday's bubble references Monday's. Square 1:1. Midjourney prompt: 'two warm speech bubbles connected by curve, soft pastel, suggesting memory thread, flat illustration, square'.

items.title: Real personalities
items.body: Pick an AI partner who matches the mood — sharp, dry, warm, blunt. They have opinions. They push back when you're wrong. They're funny when you're not trying to be.

visual.label: [Feature visual placeholder]
visual.spec: 4 character portraits in a 2×2 grid, distinct expressions. Square 1:1 overall, each portrait ~200px. Midjourney prompt: 'four illustrated character portraits, distinct personalities — wry, warm, sharp, blunt — 2x2 grid, flat illustration, square'.

demo.title: 10 seconds is enough.
demo.lead: This is the opening turn. No greeting filler, no "how can I help." Just an AI partner who's been waiting for you to walk in.

videoPlaceholder.label: [Demo video placeholder]
videoPlaceholder.spec: 30s screen-record of one real conversation: open app → AI talks first → user replies → AI references something from a prior session → end. 9:16 portrait (1080×1920) — matches the iPhone aspect the visitor would see. Captions on. Capture via QuickTime's iPhone screen-record (Phone connected → File → New Movie Recording → camera/source = iPhone) or directly on the device via Settings → Control Center → Screen Recording. Host on /public/video/demo.mp4 + a poster frame on /public/video/demo-poster.jpg.

trust.title: Built by someone who lived this for two decades.
trust.body: Stuck-functional English isn't a hypothesis. It's the founder's daily reality across 20+ years of meetings, calls, and side-comments missed. Every design choice on this product comes from a notebook of "why does this still suck."

pricing.teaserTitle: Free to start. Cheaper than the rest.
pricing.pageTitle: Free to start. One price across the app and the web.
pricing.pageLead: Most people start with the free {day} min/day ({week} min/week). When you're ready for more, $12.99/month — less than a habit you already pay for.
pricing.dualCtaNote: Either route works — same account, same access.

faq.title: Things people ask before they install.

items.a: No, it needs a connection. Your AI partner runs in the cloud, so if you're underground or on a plane without wifi, it pauses until you're back online.
Transcripts and audio are stored under your account so your AI partner remembers you next time. You can opt out of training use during signup.

items.q: Can I use it on AirPods during my commute?
items.a: Yes. Bluetooth audio works just like the phone speaker. The walk from the train station is one of the most common ways people use it.
No. Your AI partner adapts its speaking speed and corrects gently, only when you ask or when a mistake is something a friend would actually flag. You set the proficiency level; it respects it.
iPhone only for now. If you're on Android, join the waitlist and we'll email you when there's something for you.
Yes. Cancel anytime; access continues until the end of the current billing period.

waitlist.metaDescription: Be the first to know when Palkie Talkie launches.
waitlist.title: Join the waitlist.
waitlist.lead: The app isn't on the App Store yet. Tell us where to find you, and we'll let you know the day it lands.

fields.name: First name
fields.multiSelectHint: Tap to pick one or more.
fields.biggestPain: What's hardest about speaking right now? (optional)
fields.biggestPainPlaceholder: Phone calls. I freeze every time.

waitlist.thanksLead: We'll email you the day it launches — and a couple of times before that with what we're learning, if you don't mind.

footer.ctaLead: Free {day} min/day, {week} min/week. Subscribe whenever you're ready.

signup.metaDescription: Subscribe to Palkie Talkie Individual or Family on the web.
signup.canceled: Checkout canceled. You can try again any time.

signup.cancelNote: Cancel anytime. Access remains until the end of the billing period.

success.metaDescription: Subscription confirmed. Download the app to start talking.
success.title: Now download the app.
success.lead: Your subscription is active. The app is where Palkie Talkie lives.
success.instructions: Sign in with the same email you used here.
success.note: That's how we link your subscription to your account.
